title: Mildly Tangy Korean Corn Ssam
description: A tangy and aerated Korean dish featuring corn flour and pasta water emulsion, served in a salt block vessel, perfect for kids and vegan friendly.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together corn flour, rice vinegar, gochujang, sesame oil, salt, and sugar until smooth.
Add pasta water to the bowl and whisk until a thick emulsion forms.
Stir in chopped scallions and toasted sesame seeds.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until it reaches 165 degrees Fahrenheit.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
Prepare the salt block vessel by rinsing it with water and drying it with a towel.
Spoon the cooled corn mixture onto the salt block vessel.
Garnish with additional scallions and sesame seeds if desired.
Serve the ssam in lettuce leaves and enjoy.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
